page: Amazon Web Services i7i.16xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8559C processors. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance provides 64 virtual CPUs across 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 2.4 GHz. The CPU features a 320 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) for accelerated matrix and vector operations. The server is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core.

A defining feature of the i7i.16xlarge is its bundled local storage, consisting of 15,000 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. This high-capacity local storage is paired with a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The instance does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates strong to top-tier performance across several domains. In computational tests, the server achieved top-tier scores in Geekbench single-core and multi-core evaluations, as well as Passmark CPU physics tests. Memory performance is highly competitive, with DDR5 memory bandwidth registering in the top 25% for both cached small-block and uncached large-block reads. Database operations and static web serving throughput both ranked in the top 10% tier. For machine learning workloads, the CPU's AMX instructions support strong prompt processing and text generation speeds for small and medium LLM models, though text generation for large 70B models drops to average performance.

Given its dense 15 TB NVMe SSD storage and 512 GB DDR5 RAM, the i7i.16xlarge offers a highly resource-dense profile for data-intensive workloads. The qualitative cost efficiency is optimized for scenarios requiring massive local storage throughput alongside high-speed memory access, avoiding the network latency of external storage. This makes the server highly suitable for large-scale relational and non-relational databases, high-throughput static web serving, data warehousing, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services i7i.16xlarge is a storage-optimized, x86_64 server designed for data-intensive workloads. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor running at 2.4 GHz, backed by 512.0 GB of DDR5 memory. The instance includes 15,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps, managed by the Nitro hypervisor. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in database operations, static web serving, and CPU physics, alongside strong memory bandwidth and multi-core processing. The processor's support for Intel AMX enables strong performance in small-to-medium LLM inference. This hardware configuration makes the i7i.16xlarge highly suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, and enterprise applications requiring massive local storage and high memory capacity.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7i.16xlarge is a storage-optimized server with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR5 RAM, and 15,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier database and web serving performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i7i.16xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 64 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR5 RAM, and 15,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-capacity storage and memory-optimized server featuring 15 TB of local NVMe SSDs and advanced Intel Xeon processing.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8559C processors with AMX support
512 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
15,000 GB of high-performance local NVMe SSD storage
38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier benchmark performance in database operations and static web serving
Best suited for large-scale databases, data warehousing, and enterprise applications
